text:
Admission Date:  2018-11-10              Discharge Date:   2004-07-05

Date of Birth:  2005-04-07             Sex:   F

Service: NEUROSURGERY

Allergies:
Penicillins / Sulfa (Sulfonamide Antibiotics)

Attending:Latoya
Chief Complaint:
Severe headache

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
06-06 CTA brain no aneurysm
07-09 Negative Angiography
02-05 CTA brain no aneurysm,bleed, infarct or ischemia
2017-06-19 Diagnostic Angiography


History of Present Illness:
51y/o female who experienced onset of severe occipital headache
at 2:30pm on date of admission.  She was leaving her house to
walk her dog and was unable to complete the walk due to pain.
She called EMS after pain did not subside and was seen at OSH.
No complaint of photophobia or visual changes, no neck pain, no
nausea/vomiting. CT done there showed SAH with blood in
pre-pontine cistern, ? small amt in 4th vent.

Past Medical History:
None

Social History:
+30pk/yr smoking, 2-3drinks/day(3-4nights per week)


Family History:
No h/o aneurysms, mother with ?TIAs in 70s-80s ROS: No h/o
bleeding disorders.  Occasional migraine h/a and tension h/a...
none similar to current h/a.

Physical Exam:
On Admission:
T: 98.5  BP: 154/6 ->current SBP 125 HR: 70 R16   O2Sats 96ra
Gen: WD/WN, comfortable, NAD.
HEENT: Pupils:2mm, PERRLA   EOMs intact
Neck: Supple. no nuchal rigidity
Lungs: CTA bilaterally.
Cardiac: RRR. S1/S2.
Abd: Soft, NT, BS+
Extrem: Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro:
Mental status: Awake and alert, cooperative with exam, normal
affect.
Orientation: Oriented to person, place, and date.
Language: Speech fluent with good comprehension and repetition.
Naming intact. No dysarthria or paraphasic errors.

Cranial Nerves:
I: Not tested
II: Pupils equally round and reactive to light, 2mm bilaterally.
Visual fields are full to confrontation.
III, IV, VI: Extraocular movements intact bilaterally without
nystagmus.
V, VII: Facial strength and sensation intact and symmetric.
VIII: Hearing intact to voice.
IX, X: Palatal elevation symmetrical.
Tiffany: Sternocleidomastoid and trapezius normal bilaterally.
XII: Tongue midline without fasciculations.

Motor: Normal bulk and tone bilaterally. No abnormal movements,
tremors. Strength full power 11-05 throughout. No pronator drift

Sensation: Intact to light touch, propioception, pinprick and
vibration bilaterally.

Toes downgoing bilaterally

Coordination: normal on finger-nose-finger, rapid alternating
movements

On Discharge:
XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX

Pertinent Results:
Labs on Admisson:
2018-11-10 06:35PM BLOOD WBC-8.0 RBC-4.14* Hgb-13.3 Hct-37.7
MCV-91 MCH-32.2* MCHC-35.4* RDW-13.0 Plt Ct-341
2018-11-10 06:35PM BLOOD Neuts-76.2* Lymphs-20.6 Monos-2.1 Eos-0.8
Baso-0.2
2018-11-10 06:35PM BLOOD PT-12.1 PTT-22.6 INR(PT)-1.0
2018-11-10 06:35PM BLOOD Glucose-108* UreaN-6 Creat-0.7 Na-140
K-4.0 Cl-107 HCO3-22 AnGap-15

Labs on Discharge:
XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX

Imaging:


Brief Hospital Course:
Patient was admitted to neurosurgery service at The Burdett Care Center after
being transferred from OSH for evaluation for SAH.  CT at the
OSH reportedly revealed atraumtic SAH in the prepontine
cisterns, however re-imaging at The Burdett Care Center did not reveal this.  A
CTA of the head was done in the emergency department which
showed: No aneurysms.  She was then admitted to the ICU with
strict SBP control, started on nimodipine pending a diagnostic
angiogram the following morning. On 07-09 she underwent
angiogram which revealed again, no aneurysm  to account for the
Subarachnoid bleeding. Her neurologic exam remains nonfocal. CTA
done on 02-05 showing no new bleeding/spasm/infarct or ischemia.
On 12-17 she had a chest xray that did not reveal any pulmonary
processes.07-23 one additional angio was done and resulting in
no identification of any aneurysms. She is progressing as
expected and will follow up with Dr. Michael in one month.

Medications on Admission:
None

Discharge Medications:
1. Senna 8.6 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a
day).
2. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID (2
times a day).
3. Bisacodyl 5 mg Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) Sig: Two (2)
Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) PO DAILY (Daily) as needed.
4. Butalbital-Acetaminophen-Caff 50-325-40 mg Tablet Sig: 07-01
Tablets PO Q4H (every 4 hours) as needed.
5. Phenytoin Sodium Extended 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule
PO TID (3 times a day).
6. Famotidine 20 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a
day).
7. Nimodipine 30 mg Capsule Sig: Two (2) Capsule PO Q4H (every 4
hours).


Discharge Disposition:
Home

Discharge Diagnosis:
Atraumatic Subarachnoid hemorrhage


Discharge Condition:
Neurologically stable.


Discharge Instructions:
Angiogram with Embolization and/or Stent placement
Medications:
The Nimodipine will continue until 03-04 and the Dilantin will
continue for one month. Do not stop abruptly.
??????	Continue all other medications you were taking before surgery,
unless otherwise directed
??????	You make take Tylenol or prescribed pain medications for any
post procedure pain or discomfort

What activities you can and cannot do:
??????	When you go home, you may walk and go up and down stairs
??????	You may shower (let the soapy water run over groin incision,
rinse and pat dry)
??????	Your incision may be left uncovered, unless you have small
amounts of drainage from the wound, then place a dry dressing or
band aid over the area that is draining, as needed
??????	No heavy lifting, pushing or pulling (greater than 5 lbs) for
1 week (to allow groin puncture to heal)
??????	After 1 week, you may resume sexual activity
??????	After 1 week, gradually increase your activities and distance
walked as you can tolerate
??????	No driving until you are no longer taking pain medications

What to report to office:
??????	Changes in vision (loss of vision, blurring, double vision,
half vision)
??????	Slurring of speech or difficulty finding correct words to use
??????	Severe headache or worsening headache not controlled by pain
medication
??????	A sudden change in the ability to move or use your arm or leg
or the ability to feel your arm or leg
??????	Trouble swallowing, breathing, or talking
??????	Numbness, coldness or pain in lower extremities
??????	Temperature greater than 101.5F for 24 hours
??????	New or increased drainage from incision or white, yellow or
green drainage from incisions
??????	Bleeding from groin puncture site

*SUDDEN, SEVERE BLEEDING OR SWELLING
(Groin puncture site)
Lie down, keep leg straight and have someone apply firm pressure
to area for 10 minutes. If bleeding stops, call our office. If
bleeding does not stop, call 911 for transfer to closest
Emergency Room

Followup Instructions:
Please call 850-756-9741 to schedule a follow up appointment to
be seen by Dr. Michael in approximately 4 weeks.
